message:
feat: pass KUBECONFIG env var to kc-agent in startup scripts (#3166)

Both startup scripts already source .env, but kc-agent was launched
without forwarding the KUBECONFIG variable. Now if KUBECONFIG is set
(via .env or shell environment), it is passed as --kubeconfig to
kc-agent so users can configure a custom kubeconfig path.

Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -152,7 +152,11 @@ fi
 # Start kc-agent
 if command -v kc-agent &>/dev/null; then
     echo "Starting kc-agent..."
-    kc-agent &
+    KC_AGENT_ARGS=()
+    if [ -n "$KUBECONFIG" ]; then
+        KC_AGENT_ARGS+=(--kubeconfig "$KUBECONFIG")
+    fi
+    kc-agent "${KC_AGENT_ARGS[@]}" &
     AGENT_PID=$!
     sleep 2
 else

@@ -327,8 +327,13 @@ AGENT_LOOP_PID=""
 if [ -n "$KC_AGENT_BIN" ]; then
     echo -e "${GREEN}Starting kc-agent ($KC_AGENT_BIN)...${NC}"
     (
+        # Pass KUBECONFIG from .env / environment as --kubeconfig flag
+        KC_AGENT_ARGS=()
+        if [ -n "$KUBECONFIG" ]; then
+            KC_AGENT_ARGS+=(--kubeconfig "$KUBECONFIG")
+        fi
         while true; do
-            "$KC_AGENT_BIN" &
+            "$KC_AGENT_BIN" "${KC_AGENT_ARGS[@]}" &
             CHILD=$!
             echo "[kc-agent] Started (PID $CHILD)"
             wait $CHILD
